name: coding-raw
description: Single-stage baseline: one agent plans, implements, and self-checks a code change in one session, then opens a PR. No plan/implement/verify decomposition and no adversarial verify loop — the raw-agent control against which the staged `coding` workflow is measured (see evals/agent-vs-workflow.eval.ts).

stages.prompt: Implement the runtime task end to end in this one session. Study the repository first (read/grep/find; check ctx_search and search_fleet_knowledge for prior work on this area), then make the code changes following repo conventions, then self-check your work: run the repo's build/test/lint via bash and confirm git diff --cached --stat looks right. Include that diff stat in your analysis. You are the only agent on this task — there is no separate planner or verifier, so hold yourself to the same bar: no missed requirements, no regressions in touched paths, conventions followed. When the change is complete and checked, submit_work with your analysis and control {"status":"done"}.
